$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to artisanpack namespace; parent rewired from core/columns to artisanpack/columns; attributes/supports verbatim.
TypeScript port. Adds explicit 'wp-block-column' class to useBlockProps so editor matches frontend.
TypeScript port; class list adds 'wp-block-column' explicitly.
Single v1 entry ported verbatim under the artisanpack namespace.
Upstream ships no column/transforms.js — column is child-only. Added bidirectional core/column ↔ artisanpack/column block transforms so mixed documents survive paste/conversion during the V2 rollout.
Editor-only stylesheet. Duplicates the upstream columns/editor.scss margin reset so the column block has a scoped editor stylesheet; runtime layout still lives in artisanpack/columns columns.css.
Inline SVG for the inserter (editor doesn't bundle dashicons).
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract. Upstream's PHP-side init wiring is not carried.

knownDivergences: block.json parent rewired from core/columns to artisanpack/columns — required so the fork only nests inside artisanpack/columns.
edit.tsx + save.tsx add 'wp-block-column' to useBlockProps explicitly; upstream relies on __experimentalSelector to inject it.
transforms.ts is added in the fork (upstream ships none) to provide bidirectional core/column ↔ artisanpack/column block transforms during the V2 rollout.
column.css is added in the fork (upstream ships no style.scss for column). Layout rules still live in artisanpack/columns columns.css.
Native files (edit.native.js, column-preview.native.js, editor.native.scss) are intentionally not ported — desktop web only.
